user.title: Select OneControl Gateway
user.description: Choose a discovered OneControl BLE gateway to set up.

pairing_method.title: Gateway Type — {name}
pairing_method.description:
Does your OneControl gateway have a physical **Connect** button you press to start pairing, or does it use a fixed PIN printed on a sticker?

Check the label on your gateway if you're unsure.

confirm.title: Configure {name}
confirm.description: Enter the 6-digit PIN from the sticker on your OneControl gateway.

confirm_pin.title: Configure {name} (PIN Gateway)
confirm_pin.description:
This is a PIN-based OneControl gateway. Your gateway PIN is used for Bluetooth pairing and/or protocol authentication depending on gateway family.

**Note:** PIN-based gateways work best with a direct USB Bluetooth adapter. ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy support is untested and may not work.

data.bluetooth_pin: Bluetooth PIN override (leave blank to use Gateway PIN)

confirm_x180t.title: Configure {name} (Unity X180T Gateway)
confirm_x180t.description: This gateway matches the official Unity X180T CAN-BLE profile. If your gateway label or control panel shows a 6-digit Bluetooth PIN, enter it here. The CAN connection itself uses the official app's empty X180T password handling.

abort.no_devices_found: No OneControl gateways found. Make sure Bluetooth is enabled and the gateway is powered on.
